data.title: Implementation plan creates oversized triage tasks that exceed sub-agent context limits
data.description:
## Description
When /dso:implementation-plan creates a TRIVIAL story's task (or when /dso:sprint creates a task for a TRIVIAL story that skipped implementation-plan), analysis tasks that span many files (e.g., 'triage 76 test files') are created as a single task. This exceeds sub-agent context capacity, causing auto-compaction and context rot during execution.

## Root Cause
The TRIVIAL classification skips /dso:implementation-plan entirely, so there is no Gate 3 (maximum granularity) check or file-count-based splitting. The sprint orchestrator creates a single task from the story description verbatim, regardless of the number of files involved.

## Expected Behavior
When a TRIVIAL story's scope involves analyzing or modifying more than ~30 files, the task should be automatically split into smaller subsets (e.g., by directory or alphabetical range) so each sub-agent stays within context limits.

## Reproduction
1. Create a story classified as TRIVIAL(high) that involves triaging 76 test files
2. Sprint creates a single task for the entire scope
3. Sub-agent dispatched on the task runs out of context

## Suggested Fix
Add a file-count heuristic in sprint Phase 4 (Sub-Agent Launch) or in implementation-plan's TRIVIAL skip path: when the task's file impact exceeds a threshold (~30 files), split the dispatch into parallel sub-agents with subset scopes.
